response:
The heavyweight boxer is famous for his incredible strength.
He fights with an aggressive style that intimidates opponents.
This specific boxer has faced a professional rival in three matches.
The first match was a highly anticipated event for fans.
In that initial bout, he landed exactly one hundred twenty power punches.
These punches were delivered with tremendous force and speed.
The crowd was amazed by the sheer volume of strikes.
The second match took place after a period of intense training.
He decided to increase his output of power punches significantly.
He managed to boost his number of power punches by twenty-five percent.
To find the new total, we must calculate twenty-five percent of one hundred twenty.
Twenty-five percent is equivalent to one quarter of the original number.
One quarter of one hundred twenty equals thirty additional punches.
Adding thirty to the original one hundred twenty gives us one hundred fifty.
So, in the second match, he landed one hundred fifty power punches.
The third match was the most exciting of the entire series.
He continued to improve his performance in the ring.
During this third encounter, he landed thirty more punches than before.
We need to add thirty to the total from the second match.
One hundred fifty plus thirty equals one hundred eighty power punches.
Therefore, he landed one hundred eighty power punches in the third match.
Now we must calculate the total across all three separate matches.
We start by adding the first match total of one hundred twenty.
Then we add the second match total of one hundred fifty.
Next, we add the third match total of one hundred eighty.
The sum of one hundred twenty and one hundred fifty is two hundred seventy.
Adding one hundred eighty to two hundred seventy gives us four hundred fifty.
The final calculation shows he landed four hundred fifty power punches.
This total demonstrates his consistent power throughout the series.
His aggressive style clearly paid off in every single fight.
Fans will remember this series as a testament to his skill.
The numbers prove that he was the dominant force in the ring.
